site.copyright: © 2026 Deckyard. Open source under MIT license.

footer.tagline: Open source presentations with live interaction.

meta.title: Deckyard — The presentation platform that respects your data
meta.description: EU-hosted, GDPR-native, self-hostable presentation platform. 44 slide types, built-in polls and Q&A, AI generation with your own keys. MIT licensed and open source.

hero.title: The presentation platform that respects your data
hero.lead: EU-hosted, GDPR-compliant by design, and fully self-hostable. Create professional presentations with 44 slide types, built-in polls and Q&A, and AI generation using your own API keys.
hero.screenshotAlt: Deckyard editor showing slide types, live preview, and presenter notes

features.title: Everything you need to create professional presentations
features.lead: Deckyard isn't a toy—it's a complete presentation platform with 44 slide types covering everything from data visualizations to process diagrams to image galleries.

data.title: Data & Visualizations
data.description: Charts, KPIs, tables, funnels, pyramids, SWOT matrices. Present your numbers with clarity.

layouts.title: Layouts & Structure
layouts.description: Section titles, text slides, multi-column layouts, icon cards, timelines, roadmaps. Build narrative flow.

media.title: Media & Embeds
media.description: Images, galleries, video embeds, maps, forms. Rich media without friction.

export.title: Export Anywhere
export.description: HTML for web, PDF for print, PPTX for PowerPoint, PNG for images, JSON for data portability.

interaction.title: Engage your audience without switching tools
interaction.lead: Polls, Q&A, Likert scales, and feedback forms—built directly into your slides. No more juggling Mentimeter alongside your presentation.

realtime.title: Real-time audience interaction
realtime.livePollsDesc: Multiple choice with instant results display
realtime.qnaDesc: Collect questions, display and answer live

participation.title: Simple audience participation
participation.qrCode: Audience joins via QR code or short link
participation.noApp: No app download required—works in any browser
participation.followAlong: Follow-along mode so attendees see current slide
participation.resultsRealtime: Results appear in real-time on your slides

interaction.compareToMentimeter: See how we compare to Mentimeter →

ai.title: AI that works for you, not against your wallet
ai.lead: Generate entire presentations from a prompt. Use your own API keys—OpenAI, Anthropic, Mistral, DeepSeek, or any OpenAI-compatible model. No credits, no limits, no surprises on your bill.

byoKeys.title: Bring your own keys
byoKeys.description: Connect your OpenAI, Anthropic, Mistral, DeepSeek, or self-hosted model. Pay your provider directly at their rates—or run local models for free. No markup, no credits to run out.

fullDeck.title: Full deck generation
fullDeck.description: Describe your presentation and let AI create a complete first draft with appropriate slide types, content, and structure.

control.title: You're in control
control.description: AI assists—you decide. Every suggestion is reviewable. Nothing changes without your approval. DreamBot analyzes your slides and suggests improvements, but you make the call.

whyByo.title: Why BYO API keys?
whyByo.description: Other tools charge "AI credits" on top of your subscription. Run out? Pay more. With Deckyard, you pay your AI provider directly at their rates.

sovereignty.title: Your infrastructure, your rules
sovereignty.lead: Run Deckyard on your own servers with Docker, or use our managed EU cloud. Either way, your data stays where you want it.

selfHost.title: Self-host (always free)
selfHost.fullControl: Full control over data and infrastructure
selfHost.scale: Scale as needed, pay only for your servers

opensource.title: Open source. No tricks.
opensource.lead: Deckyard is MIT licensed. View the source, audit the code, fork it, extend it, self-host it forever. We believe the best way to earn your trust is to be completely transparent.

noLockin.title: No vendor lock-in
noLockin.description: If we disappear tomorrow, you still have a working product. Your presentations are never held hostage.

inspect.title: Inspect everything
inspect.description: Security-conscious? Read every line of code. We have nothing to hide.

community.title: Community-driven
community.description: Report bugs, request features, contribute code. Help shape the roadmap.

enterprise.title: Enterprise-friendly
enterprise.description: MIT license means no legal headaches for your procurement team.

cta.title: Ready to own your presentations?
cta.lead: Try the sandbox with no signup, or deploy on your own infrastructure in minutes.

meta.title: Self-hosting Deckyard — Run on your own infrastructure
meta.description: Deploy Deckyard on your own servers with Docker. Complete data control, no external dependencies, 5 minute setup.

hero.title: Run Deckyard on your own infrastructure
hero.lead: Complete data control. No external dependencies. Deploy with Docker in 5 minutes.

quickstart.title: Up and running in 5 minutes
quickstart.docker: Docker & Docker Compose — Required for containerized deployment
quickstart.postgres: PostgreSQL 14+ — Included in compose, or use external database
quickstart.s3: Optional: S3-compatible storage for media files

sovereignty.title: Complete data sovereignty
sovereignty.description: Your presentation data never leaves your servers. No third-party access, no data mining, no surprises.

compliance.title: Compliance requirements
compliance.description: Healthcare, government, education, finance—many organizations require on-premise solutions.

cost.title: Cost control at scale
cost.description: No per-seat pricing anxiety. One deployment serves your entire organization.

customization.title: Customization freedom
customization.description: Modify the source, add integrations, adjust the UI. MIT license means no restrictions.

config.title: Configuration
config.aiDesc: Connect your own API keys to enable AI features. You only pay your provider directly—no markup.

cta.title: Ready to deploy?
cta.lead: Get Deckyard running on your infrastructure in minutes.

meta.title: EU Hosting & GDPR — Deckyard Privacy
meta.description: Deckyard is hosted in the EU on French infrastructure. GDPR compliant by design, with data sovereignty and transparent data practices.

hero.title: EU-hosted. GDPR by design.
hero.lead: Your data stays in Europe. On French infrastructure. Under EU law.

hostedInFrance.title: Hosted in France
hostedInFrance.description: Our managed cloud runs on Scaleway infrastructure in France. Your data never leaves the EU.

gdprCompliant.title: GDPR compliant
gdprCompliant.description: Built by an EU company (Netherlands) for EU compliance from day one. No US cloud providers in the chain.

noTracking.title: No tracking, no analytics
noTracking.description: We don't track your usage. No Google Analytics, no marketing pixels, no behavioral profiling.

yourKeys.title: Your keys, your AI
yourKeys.description: AI features use your own API keys. We never see your prompts or store your AI conversations.

dataProcessing.title: Data processing details
dataProcessing.noIpAddresses: IP addresses of your audience members
dataProcessing.noAiPrompts: AI prompts or generated content (processed directly with your API keys)

selfHosting.title: Self-hosting: complete control
selfHosting.lead: For organizations with strict data requirements, Deckyard can run entirely on your own infrastructure:
selfHosting.airGapped: Air-gapped deployments — No external network calls required
selfHosting.onPremise: On-premise databases — PostgreSQL on your servers
selfHosting.internalAi: Internal AI models — Connect to self-hosted LLMs
selfHosting.audit: Audit everything — Full source code available under MIT license

dpa.question: Can I get a DPA (Data Processing Agreement)?
dpa.answer: Yes. Contact us at privacy@deckyard.eu for our standard DPA or to discuss custom requirements.

cloudAct.question: Is Deckyard subject to US CLOUD Act?
cloudAct.answer: No. Deckyard is operated by from the Netherlands. We use only EU infrastructure providers (Scaleway). There are no US entities in our data processing chain.

audienceData.question: What happens to audience response data?
audienceData.answer: Audience responses (poll votes, Q&A submissions, etc.) are stored with your presentation. They're deleted when you delete the presentation. We don't analyze or profile audience behavior.

aiGdpr.question: Do AI features comply with GDPR?
aiGdpr.answer: AI features use your own API keys, meaning prompts go directly to your chosen provider (OpenAI, Anthropic, etc.). We don't process or store AI interactions. You maintain your own relationship and DPA with your AI provider.

exportData.question: Can I export all my data?
exportData.answer: Yes. You can export presentations as JSON, and audience data as CSV. For complete account data exports (GDPR Article 20), contact us.

deleteAccount.question: How do I delete my account?
deleteAccount.answer: Account deletion is available in settings. All your data is permanently removed within 30 days. For immediate deletion requests, contact privacy@deckyard.eu.

cta.title: Questions about privacy?
cta.lead: We're happy to discuss your specific requirements.

meta.title: Pricing — Deckyard
meta.description: Simple pricing. No per-seat anxiety. Self-host for free, or use our managed EU cloud with Pro and Team plans.

hero.title: Simple pricing. No per-seat anxiety.
hero.lead: Self-host for free forever, or let us handle the infrastructure. All features included in every plan.

selfHostedIncludes.question: What's included in self-hosted?
selfHostedIncludes.answer: Everything. Self-hosted Deckyard includes all 44 slide types, all interactive features (polls, Q&A, feedback), AI generation, export to all formats, and unlimited presentations and audience members. The only difference is you manage your own infrastructure.

aiCosts.question: Do I pay for AI features?
aiCosts.answer: AI features use your own API keys from OpenAI, Anthropic, Mistral, DeepSeek, or any OpenAI-compatible service. You pay your provider directly at their rates—typically €0.02-0.05 per deck generation. Self-hosted models (via Ollama, vLLM, etc.) are free. We don't mark up API costs or charge AI credits.

enterprise.question: Is there an enterprise tier?
enterprise.answer: Not yet. For larger teams or specific compliance requirements, contact us to discuss custom arrangements. We're happy to work with you on dedicated hosting, custom SLAs, or specific security requirements.

switchPlans.question: Can I switch between plans?
switchPlans.answer: Yes. You can upgrade, downgrade, or switch to self-hosting at any time. Your data is always exportable in standard formats (JSON, PDF, PPTX).

paymentMethods.question: What payment methods do you accept?
paymentMethods.answer: We accept credit cards and SEPA direct debit for EU customers. Annual billing is available with a 15% discount.

discounts.question: Do you offer discounts?
discounts.answer: We offer 50% off for non-profits and educational institutions. Contact us with proof of status to apply.

compare.title: Compare to alternatives
compare.lead: See how Deckyard pricing compares to other presentation tools.

cta.title: Ready to get started?
cta.lead: Try the sandbox with no signup, or deploy on your own infrastructure.

meta.title: Deckyard vs Mentimeter — Open Source Alternative
meta.description: Compare Deckyard and Mentimeter for audience interaction. Open source, self-hostable, no per-seat pricing, EU-hosted alternative.

hero.title: Deckyard vs Mentimeter
hero.lead: Love audience interaction, but want to own your tools? Deckyard gives you polls, Q&A, and live feedback—plus a full presentation editor.

pricing.title: 1. No more per-seat pricing anxiety
pricing.description: Mentimeter charges per presenter. At $11.99/month basic or $24.99/month pro, a 20-person team costs $240–$500/month. Deckyard's flat pricing (or free self-hosting) means your costs don't scale with headcount.

fullEditor.title: 2. Build entire presentations, not just interactions
fullEditor.description: Mentimeter excels at interactive slides but lacks a full presentation editor. You end up bouncing between Mentimeter and PowerPoint. Deckyard combines both: design complete decks with 44 slide types, then add live interaction where you need it.

dataSovereignty.title: 3. Real data sovereignty
dataSovereignty.description: Mentimeter is a Swedish company but operates US infrastructure. For GDPR-conscious organizations, that's a concern. Deckyard runs on French servers, or you can self-host entirely on your own infrastructure.

byoAi.title: 4. BYO AI, no markup
byoAi.description: Mentimeter's AI features come with their paid plans. Deckyard lets you connect your own API keys—OpenAI, Anthropic, Mistral, DeepSeek, or any OpenAI-compatible model including self-hosted. You pay only what you use, at provider rates.

openSource.title: 5. Open source transparency
openSource.description: Deckyard's code is public under MIT license. You can audit it, modify it, contribute to it. No black boxes, no vendor lock-in, no surprise deprecations.

whenMentimeter.title: When to stick with Mentimeter
whenMentimeter.lead: We're honest about trade-offs. Mentimeter might be better if:
whenMentimeter.turnkey: You need turnkey simplicity — Mentimeter's SaaS model means zero setup. If you have no technical resources and don't want to manage anything, that's valuable.
whenMentimeter.soloPresenter: You're a solo presenter — For individuals giving occasional talks, Mentimeter's free tier (50 participants) may be sufficient.
whenMentimeter.integrations: You need specific integrations — Mentimeter has more third-party integrations built in. Deckyard is catching up, but check if your specific tool is supported.

migration.title: Migration is simple
migration.step1: Export your content — Download your Mentimeter presentations as PDFs for reference
migration.step2: Recreate in Deckyard — Use our AI generator to quickly rebuild decks, or import content manually
migration.step3: Test with your audience — Run a pilot session to ensure everything works
migration.step4: Go live — Share your new Deckyard presentation URL with participants

cta.title: Ready to try the open alternative?
cta.lead: See how Deckyard handles your audience interaction needs.

meta.title: Deckyard vs Slides.com — Open Source Presentation Tool
meta.description: Compare Deckyard and Slides.com for presentation creation. Open source, self-hostable, with live audience interaction built in.

hero.title: Deckyard vs Slides.com
hero.lead: Both are modern web-based presentation tools. But Deckyard adds live audience interaction and lets you self-host.

reasons.title: Why teams choose Deckyard over Slides.com

audienceInteraction.title: 1. Audience interaction is built in
audienceInteraction.description: Slides.com creates beautiful presentations, but there's no way for your audience to participate. Deckyard includes live polls, Q&A, word clouds, Likert scales, and feedback collection—all in one tool.

selfHost.title: 2. Self-host for complete control
selfHost.description: Slides.com is cloud-only. If you need to run presentations on internal networks, meet compliance requirements, or simply own your infrastructure, Deckyard's Docker deployment makes it possible.

aiPowered.title: 3. AI-powered deck generation
aiPowered.description: Upload a document, get a presentation. Deckyard's AI features help you create decks faster using your own API keys (OpenAI, Anthropic, Mistral). Slides.com has no AI capabilities.

openSource.title: 4. Open source = no lock-in
openSource.description: If Slides.com changes pricing, removes features, or shuts down, you lose access. Deckyard's MIT-licensed code means you can always fork it, modify it, and run it yourself.

euData.title: 5. EU data residency
euData.description: Slides.com runs on US infrastructure. Deckyard's managed cloud is hosted in France. For EU organizations, that's a meaningful difference for GDPR compliance.

whenSlidescom.title: When Slides.com makes sense
whenSlidescom.lead: Fair comparisons require honesty. Slides.com might be better if:
whenSlidescom.revealjs: You love Reveal.js and want its full power — Slides.com is built directly on Reveal.js with deep integration. If you're a Reveal.js power user, their editor is purpose-built for it.
whenSlidescom.staticOnly: You only need static presentations — If you never need audience interaction, Slides.com is simpler and more focused.
whenSlidescom.collaboration: You want mature collaboration features — Slides.com has had years to refine team collaboration. Deckyard's collaboration features are newer.

migration.title: Switching from Slides.com
migration.lead: Both tools can export to standard formats, making migration straightforward:
migration.step1: Export from Slides.com — Download your presentations as HTML or PDF
migration.step2: Import content to Deckyard — Paste content or use AI to regenerate slides from your source material
migration.step3: Add interactivity — Enhance your static slides with polls, Q&A, and audience feedback
migration.step4: Publish and share — Your new Deckyard presentations work on any device

cta.title: Try Deckyard today
cta.lead: See what interactive presentations can do for your audience engagement.
